steps.title: Identify the structure requiring completion
steps.content: The phrase is "respondents were more likely ____." After a linking verb and adjective, a specific verb form is needed.

steps.title: Apply the infinitive rule
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: the structure is "more likely ______ common Brazilian foods." Rule: the adjective likely takes a complement formed with to + base verb (infinitive), not a finite verb or participle.
  • Choice A — "ate": simple past finite verb; "more likely ate" is ungrammatical — likely requires an infinitive complement. ✗
  • Choice B — "will be eating": future progressive finite verb; same problem — likely doesn't take a finite verb complement. ✗
  • Choice C — "to eat": infinitive; "more likely to eat" is the correct adjective + infinitive idiom. ✓
  • Choice D — "eaten": past participle; "more likely eaten" reads as a passive remnant, not the active complement likely needs. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
